method_atom: a proof that the iterates of a nonlinear rank-two recursion relation over non-commuting generators are positive Laurent polynomials in the initial data, established by encoding each iterate as a weighted sum over configurations of a combinatorial path/lattice-path model whose manifestly non-negative terms enumerate the expansion
object_atom: the cluster variables generated by rank-two mutation/recursion relations that in the commutative limit produce rank-two cluster algebras of affine type, here in their non-commutative deformation as conjectured by Kontsevich
brief_reason: the recursion-to-positive-Laurent-polynomial argument via a bijective path-model enumeration is the transferable machinery, while the rank-two affine cluster-algebra variables are the algebra-specific object; the two are largely separable, though the path model is tailored to this recursion's structure.
